definitions.gloss_en: a terrain type or status indicating that movement through it is prohibited
definitions.gloss_jp: {通|つう}{過|か}や{移|い}{動|どう}が{禁|きん}{止|し}されている{地|ち}{形|けい}または{状|じょう}{態|たい}
definitions.explanation_en: Units may not move through impassable terrain. The designation prevents all normal movement, though some special rules may allow exceptions.
definitions.implicit_notes: 通過不可 is a standard Japanese negation compound (通過 + 不可). The same construction appears on traffic signs (通行不可 = no through traffic) and military maps.
definitions.orthography_notes: 通過: 通 (through/pass) + 過 (pass). 不可: 不 (non-) + 可 (possible/permitted). Standard negation compound. Weblio dictionary lists 通過 as a general-purpose word. JWC terrain rules use 通過不可. Not WH-specific.
definitions.game_setting_notes: In miniature wargames, impassable terrain blocks movement entirely. Example types: walls, cliffs, deep water. Units cannot enter or cross the terrain piece.

usage_jp: {通|つう}{過|か}{不|ふ}{可|か}の{地|ち}{形|けい} (impassable terrain); この{地|ち}{形|けい}は{通|つう}{過|か}{不|ふ}{可|か}です (this terrain is impassable)
comparative_notes: Contrasts with 困難地形 (difficult terrain) which imposes movement penalties rather than prohibition. 通行不可 is a close synonym used on traffic signs; 通過不可 is the wargame-standard form.

examples.japanese: {通|つう}{過|か}{不|ふ}{可|か}の{地|ち}{形|けい}には{侵|しん}{入|にゅう}できない。

cross_references.note: 地形 — terrain; 通過不可 is a terrain classification
